description_long: This is a floor-standing industrial control cabinet, typically used for housing electrical or automation equipment in urban infrastructure. The cabinet features a slanted control panel with a digital display, several colored push buttons, and indicator lights for operational control and monitoring. The upper section has a row of circular cutouts, likely for additional switches or meters, while the lower compartment is open, revealing multiple circuit breakers or relays arranged in rows. The exterior is constructed from smooth, painted metal, with a minimalist design and subtle ventilation holes along the bottom edge. A small information label is present on the right side of the control panel, and the overall structure is robust and utilitarian, suitable for public or industrial environments.
description: A robust, floor-standing metal control cabinet with a slanted control panel, digital display, push buttons, and visible circuit breakers in the lower compartment.
description_view_0: Front view showing the control panel with buttons, display, indicator lights, upper cutouts, and open lower compartment with circuit breakers.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the cabinet's depth, slanted control panel, and solid metal construction.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, solid metal back with a row of ventilation holes at the bottom.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the control panel's profile and the information label on the side.
